title: High Protein Lentil Soup
metaDescription: High protein lentil soup with 26g protein per serving — brown lentils, carrots, celery and warm spices in a rich vegetable broth. Ready in 40 minutes.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: lentil soup, high protein vegetarian soup, brown lentil soup

steps.name: Sauté the vegetables
steps.text: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add onion, carrots, and celery, cooking for 6-7 minutes until softened. Add garlic, cumin, and smoked paprika, cooking for 1 more minute.

steps.name: Add lentils and liquid
steps.text: Stir in rinsed lentils, diced tomatoes, vegetable broth, and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer uncovered for 25-30 minutes, until the lentils are tender.

steps.name: Finish the soup
steps.text: Remove the bay leaf. Stir in lemon juice, and season with salt and pepper to taste.

steps.name: Serve
steps.text: Ladle into bowls and top with fresh parsley. Serve with crusty bread if desired.

tips.question: Why use brown lentils instead of red?
tips.answer: Brown lentils hold their shape through a longer simmer, giving this soup a heartier, more textured bite than red lentils, which break down into a smoother, thicker consistency.

tips.question: Can I make this soup in a slow cooker?
tips.answer: Yes — combine all ingredients except the lemon juice and cook on low for 6-7 hours or high for 3-4 hours, then stir in lemon juice just before serving.

tips.question: Why add lemon juice at the end?
tips.answer: A splash of acid at the end brightens the whole pot and keeps the soup from tasting flat — lentil soups in particular benefit from this final adjustment.

tips.question: How long does this soup keep, and does it freeze well?
tips.answer: It keeps up to 5 days in the fridge and freezes well for up to 3 months — the flavor actually improves after a day or two in the fridge as the spices settle in.

storyIntro: Lentil soup rarely gets top billing, but a pot of brown lentils simmered with vegetables and warm spices quietly delivers more protein per serving than most people expect from a fully vegetarian soup.
It's the kind of recipe that scales up easily and tastes better the next day, which makes it one of the more practical meal-prep options on the site — not just a dinner, but four or five lunches.
storyNote: Simmer uncovered — this concentrates the broth as it reduces slightly, instead of staying thin and watery under a lid.
